specific_change: Replace the fixed 20× m5.xlarge compute fleet with scheduled Auto Scaling: maintain 20 instances during peak windows (weekdays 09:00–11:00 UTC and 14:00–16:00 UTC), scale down to 7 instances (65% reduction) during off-peak hours (weekdays 16:00–09:00 UTC and all day weekends 00:00–23:59 UTC). Similarly, stop one database replica during off-peak/weekend windows and restart it before peak. This preserves full peak capacity for the 99.99% SLA while reducing monthly spend from $18,400 to approximately $8,000, saving ~$10,400/month (56%).

conclusion.headline: Scheduled scaling saves ~$10,400/mo (56%) while preserving 99.99% peak SLA

telemetry_observations: CPU p50 is 15.8% but p95 reaches 86.8%, showing extreme bimodal utilization.
CPU p95 stddev is 25.2%, confirming sharp peak/off-peak divergence.
Application p95 latency median is 123.5ms, well within the 250ms SLA target.
Memory p95 median is 34.2% (max 73.2%), indicating headroom even at peak.
DB connections p50 median is 30 vs p95 of 111, mirroring the bimodal compute pattern.
Network throughput p95 ranges from 120 to 789 Mbps, confirming traffic swings.
infrastructure_context: Compute is 20× m5.xlarge with no auto-scaling policy — fixed capacity 24/7.
Database is db.r6g.2xlarge primary + 2 replicas, also fixed provisioning.
ALB uses least_outstanding_requests, which will naturally rebalance across fewer instances.
Total monthly cost is $18,400 ($11,200 compute, $6,400 database, $800 network).
correlation_observations: Compute cpu_p95 and db_query_p95_latency_ms correlate at r=0.751 (lag 0 min), confirming load is demand-driven and peaks are synchronized.
Compute cpu_p95 and network_throughput_p95 correlate at r=0.886 (lag 0 min), confirming traffic-driven CPU spikes with no queuing delay.

reasoning: The telemetry shows a stark bimodal pattern: CPU p50 of 15.8% indicates the fleet is nearly idle most of the time, while p95 of 86.8% shows peak windows fully utilize the 20-instance fleet. The zero-lag correlations between compute, database, and network confirm that load is externally driven by user traffic rather than by internal inefficiency, meaning off-peak capacity can be safely reduced without masking a systemic problem. Because the ALB already uses least_outstanding_requests routing, removing instances during off-peak will redistribute traffic cleanly across the remaining 7 instances. Maintaining full 20-instance capacity during the identified peak windows (weekdays 09:00–11:00 and 14:00–16:00 UTC) preserves the 99.99% SLA where it matters.

projected_state.memory_p95_pct_estimate: 65-75% during off-peak on fewer instances, ~34% during peak
projected_state.notes: Peak windows retain full 20-instance capacity so SLA is unaffected. Off-peak latency may slightly increase but remains well within SLA. A 5-minute warm-up buffer before peak windows ensures instances are healthy before traffic arrives.

cost_impact.notes: Peak hours total ~4h/day × 5 weekdays = 20h/week at full capacity (20 instances). Off-peak = 148h/week at 7 instances (65% reduction). Compute savings: ~$11,200 × 0.65 × (148/168) ≈ $6,400/mo. Database savings from stopping 1 replica off-peak: ~$2,100 × (148/168) ≈ $1,850/mo. Remaining savings from weekend network reduction. Approximate total savings ~$10,400/mo.

risk_assessment.primary_risk: Unexpected traffic spikes outside defined peak windows (e.g., flash sales, marketing campaigns) could overwhelm the reduced 7-instance fleet and breach the 250ms p95 SLA.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Layer a reactive target-tracking ASG policy (CPU target 70%) on top of the scheduled scaling so the fleet can expand within 2-3 minutes if off-peak traffic unexpectedly surges. Set CloudWatch alarms on p95 latency > 200ms and CPU > 75% during off-peak to trigger early warnings. Maintain an SNS notification for any scale-out events outside peak windows.
risk_assessment.rollback: Revert the ASG scheduled actions to restore the fixed 20-instance fleet. This is a single Terraform apply reverting the aws_autoscaling_schedule resources; no data migration or state change is involved.
risk_assessment.notes: Schedule transitions should include a 5-minute buffer before peak windows to allow new instances to pass health checks. Database replica restart should begin 10 minutes before peak to allow replication catch-up.
